tree.setup: A stationery shop near a school. The owner, Uncle Liu, believes every pen has a personality. He tests each one before selling it, leaving rows of tiny ink spirals on a notepad. Right now he's examining a new shipment of erasers with a magnifying glass. The shop smells like fresh paper.
tree.setup_zh: 学校旁边的文具店。店主刘叔叔相信每支笔都有性格。他卖之前都要试一试，在本子上留下一排排小墨水圈。现在他正用放大镜检查一批新橡皮。店里闻起来有新纸的味道。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo! nǐ xiǎng mǎi shénme?
turns.text_en: Hello! What would you like to buy?

options.text_pinyin: wǒ yào mǎi yì zhī bǐ.
options.text_en: I'd like to buy a pen.
options.feedback: Uncle Liu's face transforms. He reaches under the counter and produces three pens, each on its own little cloth.「这支写得很流畅，这支颜色深……」支 (zhī) is the measure word for pens.

Uncle Liu gestures at an entire wall of notebooks, organized by line spacing, cover color, and page count. He asks if you prefer grids or lines with the seriousness of a sommelier asking about wine preferences.
Uncle Liu winces as though you've asked a chef for their least favorite dish. He produces a pen but warns you solemnly:「这支……写得不太好。」最 + adjective means 'the most.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: zhè zhī bǐ hěn hǎo xiě. sān kuài qián.
turns.text_en: This pen writes very well. Three yuan.

options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, wǒ mǎi zhè ge.
options.feedback: Uncle Liu wraps the pen in tissue paper as though it were a calligraphy brush from the Song dynasty. He's placed it in exactly the right hands, he feels.
Uncle Liu nods patiently. He'll be here. The pen will be here. Take your time. 再 + verb means 'to do it again' or 'more.'
Uncle Liu gently returns the pen to its cloth with the gravity of a father putting a child back to bed. He'll find it a worthy owner someday.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好好用啊！这支笔会陪你很久的。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎohǎo yòng a! zhè zhī bǐ huì péi nǐ hěn jiǔ de.
turns.text_en: Use it well! This pen will be with you for a long time.

tree.cultural_note: Stationery culture is serious in China. Students and office workers often have strong preferences about pens, and stationery shops near schools do brisk business. A good pen is considered a small but meaningful tool for success.
